skill.name: git-pr-workflows-onboard
skill.description: Create role-specific onboarding plans for technical teams, including Git workflows, access setup, buddy programs, and 30/60/90-day milestones.
skill.summary: Plan structured technical onboarding for new team members.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found Markdown prose, checklist fences, and ordinary words such as description, design, and desk; no executable code, network calls, cryptographic functions, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The external command hits are Markdown inline code and checklist fences. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits are ordinary onboarding text, not code or commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is plain Markdown guidance and examples. The flagged tokens occur in prose, paths, or fenced checklists, with no shell invocation, network request, or cryptographic API present.

content.value_statement: Technical onboarding often lacks clear milestones, access steps, and team integration plans. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured plans for new engineering hires.
actual_capabilities: Builds pre-start, first-day, first-week, and 30/60/90-day onboarding plans.
Defines access setup tasks for accounts, hardware, communication tools, and development tools.
Creates Git and pull request learning paths for new engineering team members.
Suggests buddy programs, team introductions, and feedback checkpoints.
Adapts plans for remote, hybrid, on-site, senior, and lead engineering roles.
limitations: It provides planning guidance and does not create accounts or grant access.
It does not verify company policies, legal requirements, or HR compliance rules.
It references an implementation playbook file that is not included in this package.
It needs accurate role, team, location, and start-date details for useful output.

use_cases.title: Engineer Onboarding Plan
use_cases.description: Create a complete 30/60/90-day plan for a new software engineer joining a product team.

use_cases.title: Remote Team Integration
use_cases.description: Plan remote-first introductions, async communication routines, buddy support, and feedback checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Workflow Ramp
use_cases.description: Map the first code review, first PR, repository tour, and CI/CD learning steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Onboarding Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30/60/90-day onboarding plan for a new software engineer. Include pre-start tasks, Day 1 setup, Week 1 goals, and manager check-ins.

prompt_templates.title: Customize for Remote Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this onboarding plan for a remote engineer across time zones. Include async communication norms, buddy meetings, virtual introductions, and feedback loops.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Git and PR Workflows
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for engineering workflow training.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a first-month onboarding path for Git, pull requests, code review, CI/CD, and first production deployment readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Accelerated Senior Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for senior or lead roles.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an accelerated 30-day onboarding plan for a senior engineer. Include architecture review, stakeholder meetings, technical assessment, and ownership milestones.

output_examples.input: Onboard a mid-level backend engineer to a hybrid payments team starting July 15.
output_examples.output: A phased onboarding plan with pre-start access tasks, Day 1 orientation, Week 1 repository walkthroughs, first PR goals, and 30/60/90-day milestones.

output_examples.input: Create a remote onboarding checklist for a lead engineer joining a distributed platform team.
output_examples.output: A remote-ready checklist covering equipment, introductions, architecture reviews, process assessment, stakeholder meetings, and leadership milestones.

output_examples.input: Help a tech lead prepare a Git and code review ramp for a new hire.
output_examples.output: A workflow ramp covering repository orientation, branching strategy, review expectations, pairing sessions, and first contribution targets.

best_practices: Provide role, seniority, start date, location, team structure, and required tools.
Ask for measurable milestones that match the new hire's responsibilities.
Review generated access tasks with IT, security, HR, and the hiring manager.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to generate real passwords, tokens, or secret values.
Do not treat its checklists as a substitute for company policy review.
Do not request a generic plan when the role needs specialized tools or domain context.

faq.question: Does this skill create user accounts?
faq.answer: No. It creates planning checklists for account setup, but humans or approved systems must create accounts.

faq.question: Can it support non-engineering roles?
faq.answer: It can provide general onboarding structure, but its strongest content is for technical and engineering teams.

faq.question: Does it run Git commands?
faq.answer: No. It explains Git workflow onboarding steps and does not execute commands.

faq.question: Can it build remote onboarding plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes remote setup, async communication, virtual introductions, and distributed team feedback routines.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Role level, team size, start date, location, tools, reporting line, and special constraints improve the plan.

faq.question: Should security teams review the plan?
faq.answer: Yes. Security teams should review access, credentials, device setup, and production permission steps.
